title: rosmarinus eriocalyx subsp. tomentosus
intro: Rosmarinus eriocalyx subsp. tomentosus, also known as the woolly rosemary, is a subspecies of the rosemary plant native to the Mediterranean region. This herb has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Rosmarinus eriocalyx subsp. tomentosus include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cognitive function, and aiding in digestion and respiratory issues. Rosmarinus eriocalyx subsp. tomentosus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
